data.content: The full order lifecycle is: Order Created -> Payment Pending -> Payment Success -> Restaurant Accepted -> Preparing -> Ready for Pickup -> Rider Assigned -> Rider Arrived at Restaurant -> Picked Up -> On The Way -> Arriving at Customer -> Delivered. Exceptions lead to Rejected, Cancelled, or Failed states.

Customers can cancel an order without penalty only BEFORE the restaurant accepts it (usually within 60 seconds). Once the restaurant accepts and starts 'Preparing', the order cannot be cancelled by the customer. If forced, a 100% cancellation fee applies and no refund is given.

Refunds are issued automatically if a restaurant rejects the order, or if the order is cancelled before acceptance. Refunds are processed to the original payment source (Credit Card/UPI/Stripe) and reflect in 5-7 business days. For Kravix Wallet payments, refunds are instant.

ETA (Estimated Time of Arrival) is dynamically calculated using AI. It considers: Average Restaurant Preparation Time + Rider Distance to Restaurant + Distance from Restaurant to Customer + Live Traffic Data + Weather Conditions.

Payment states include: Pending (waiting for gateway), Success (money deducted), Failed (gateway rejected), Refunded, and COD (Cash on Delivery). If a payment fails but money is deducted, the gateway auto-reverses it in 3-5 days.

Cash on Delivery (COD) is available for orders up to ₹1500. Users can also pay using the Kravix Wallet for instant checkouts. You can top up the wallet using cards or UPI. Wallet cashbacks cannot be withdrawn to a bank account.
Customers can use Scheduled Delivery to order food in advance (up to 24 hours). The restaurant will receive the order and prepare it so it's fresh right before the scheduled delivery window.

Total bill includes: Item Price + GST (5% or 18% depending on restaurant type) + Packaging Charge (set by seller) + Delivery Fee (distance-based) + Platform Service Fee (₹5 flat). A detailed digital invoice is emailed after delivery.

If items are missing, food is spilled, or the wrong order is delivered, customers must raise a Dispute within 12 hours via the Help section. Uploading photos is mandatory. Our support team reviews and issues partial or full refunds to the Kravix Wallet.

Partial refunds apply when only some items in an order are missing or incorrect; the refund is calculated proportionally to the affected item's price plus its share of tax. Partial cancellations (removing one item before restaurant acceptance) are supported on select orders.

Split Payment lets customers pay part of the bill using Kravix Wallet balance and the remainder via card, UPI, or net banking in a single checkout, ensuring wallet credits are used first before charging the primary payment method.

A GST-compliant digital invoice, itemizing GST rate, taxable value, and total tax, is auto-generated and emailed after every delivered order. Business customers can add their GSTIN in checkout to claim input tax credit.

Kravix offers Instant Delivery (fastest available rider, priority dispatch, small surcharge) alongside standard delivery. Order priority is also automatically raised for orders that are running behind their promised ETA.

Customers can choose 'Pickup' at checkout to collect their order directly from the restaurant instead of home delivery, avoiding the delivery fee entirely. The restaurant notifies the customer when the order is ready.

If a payment gateway webhook confirming success arrives late, the order stays in 'Payment Pending' with an automatic retry check every 30 seconds for up to 5 minutes before the customer is prompted to retry or choose another method.

Customers earn Loyalty Points on every successful order (1 point per ₹10 spent). Points can be redeemed at checkout for discounts, subject to a minimum redemption threshold, and expire 12 months after being earned.
